raw_title: Harnessing Star Power: Sequential Political Cooptation of Entertainment Celebrities in China
raw_abstract: The integration of entertainment and politics has become an increasingly important feature of governance worldwide. Yet we know little about how states institutionally recruit celebrities to serve political purposes. This study provides the first empirical analysis of the political cooptation of entertainment celebrities in China, drawing on an original dataset of 2,898 individuals active from 1993 to 2023. We identify a sequential process of political cooptation through which the state initially offers access to regime-curated performances, which function both as material rewards and as ideological screening devices, and then appoints some of the celebrities to formal political bodies. The determinants of selection vary by institutional affiliation and leadership era. These findings extend theories of elite cooptation by showing how states convert media visibility into political capital and embed political control within popular culture as a strategy of regime resilience.
title_en: Harnessing Star Power: Sequential Political Cooptation of Entertainment Celebrities in China
title_zh: 利用明星力量：中国娱乐明星的序列政治吸纳
abstract_en: The integration of entertainment and politics has become an increasingly important feature of governance worldwide. Yet we know little about how states institutionally recruit celebrities to serve political purposes. This study provides the first empirical analysis of the political cooptation of entertainment celebrities in China, drawing on an original dataset of 2,898 individuals active from 1993 to 2023. We identify a sequential process of political cooptation through which the state initially offers access to regime-curated performances, which function both as material rewards and as ideological screening devices, and then appoints some of the celebrities to formal political bodies. The determinants of selection vary by institutional affiliation and leadership era. These findings extend theories of elite cooptation by showing how states convert media visibility into political capital and embed political control within popular culture as a strategy of regime resilience.
abstract_zh: 娱乐与政治的融合已成为全球治理日益重要的特征。然而，我们对国家如何制度性地招募名人以服务于政治目的知之甚少。本研究首次对中国娱乐明星的政治吸纳进行了实证分析，基于1993年至2023年间活跃的2,898名个体的原始数据集。我们识别出一个序列政治吸纳过程：国家首先提供参与政权策划的表演机会，这些表演既作为物质奖励，也作为意识形态筛选工具，然后任命部分明星进入正式政治机构。选择决定因素因制度隶属关系和领导时代而异。这些发现扩展了精英吸纳理论，展示了国家如何将媒体可见性转化为政治资本，并将政治控制嵌入流行文化中，作为政权韧性的策略。
plain_summary_zh: 这项研究探讨了中国政府如何利用娱乐明星来加强政治控制。研究发现，政府先让明星参加官方活动作为奖励和考验，然后挑选一些进入政治机构。不同时期和机构的选择标准不同。这显示了政府如何将明星的知名度转化为政治支持，让流行文化帮助维持政权稳定。
plain_summary_en: This study examines how the Chinese government uses entertainment celebrities to strengthen political control. It finds that the government first invites celebrities to official events as rewards and tests, then selects some to join political bodies. Selection criteria vary by time period and institution. This shows how the government turns celebrity fame into political support, using popular culture to help maintain regime stability.
research_direction_zh: 探索国家如何通过制度机制将非政治精英（如娱乐名人）纳入政治体系，以增强治理能力和政权合法性，特别是在威权或混合政体背景下。
research_direction_en: Investigating how states incorporate non-political elites (e.g., entertainment celebrities) into political systems through institutional mechanisms to enhance governance capacity and regime legitimacy, particularly in authoritarian or hybrid regimes.
